memoryArtifacts.description: Luton Hoo (The birth place of Sir Thomas Hoo) straddles the Hertfordshire and Bedfordshire borders between the towns of Harpenden and Luton. The unusual name "Hoo" is a Saxon word meaning the spur of a hill, and is more commonly associated with East Anglia.

popularStory.headline: THOMAS HOO, BARON HOO AND HASTINGS
popularStory.description:
Thomas Hoo, 1st Baron Hoo and Hastings KG (ca. 1396-1455) was a Knight of the Garter and English courtier.

Thomas was the son of Sir Thomas Hoo (ca. 1370 – Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, 23 August 1420) a …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Hoo the Elder於大約1396年出生在英國貝德福爾盧頓 胡時，他的父親Sir Thomas Hoo27歲，母親Eleanor Felton37歲。他於1428年7月1日之前與Lady Elizabeth Wychingham結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1455年2月13日在英國享年60歲，埋葬在英國素可芭特爾。

nameMeanings.name: Hoo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Wai Lam, Wing, Beng, Chin, Gan, Hing, Hui, Kee, Lai, Lai Ching, Mei-Mei.
variant Romanization of the surname   Hu  .

variants.name: Hu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Ming, Ping, Bin, Yi, Ying, Jing, Xiao, Qi, Yan, Chi.  Korean Chang, Chung, Min, Yiping, Chong, Chul, Hu, Jung Soo, Sang Hoon, Tian, Wook, Yiming.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 胡, a term referring to the ethnic minorities in ancient northern and western China: (i) from Hu (胡), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Henan province) during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) in the 26th year of the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720 BC ), people from the fief adopted 胡, the name of the state, as their surname. (ii) from Hu (胡), the name of a state (located in present-day Anhui province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu in the 15th year of the reign of Duke Ding of Lu (ruler of the state of Lu, reigned 509–495 BC ), people from the fief adopted 胡, the name of the state, as their surname. (iii) from the personal name of Hu Gong Man (胡公满, Duke Hu of Chen), the posthumous title of Yu Man. He was given the fief of Chen (located mainly in present-day Henan and Anhui province) by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). (iv) from the personal name of Hu Guang (胡廣), who changed his original surname 黃 to 胡 during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ). (v) from the Hu Gu (胡骨) or He Gu (纥骨) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their surname to 胡 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 扈, meaning ‘retinue’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Hu (扈), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Shaanxi province) dating back to the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ) (ii) adopted as a surname by the Hu Di Gan (扈地干) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Hoo the Elder was born about 1396, in Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, England, his father, Sir Thomas Hoo, was 27 and his mother, Eleanor Felton, was 37. He married Lady Elizabeth Wychingham before 1 July 1428. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 13 February 1455, in England, at the age of 60, and was buried in Battle, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Hoo the Elder est né environ 1396, à Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Hoo, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Felton, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Lady Elizabeth Wychingham avant 1 juillet 1428. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 13 février 1455, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à Battle, Sussex, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Hoo the Elder(이)가 대략 1396년 영국 베드퍼드실르 루턴 후에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Hoo(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor Felton(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1428년 7월 1일 이전 Lady Elizabeth Wychingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1455년 2월 13일 영국에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 바틀에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Lady Elizabeth Wychingham antes 1 de julho de 1428. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Hoo the Elder родился около 1396 г. в местности лутон ху, бедфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Hoo было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Felton, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Elizabeth Wychingham перед 1 июля 1428 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 13 февраля 1455 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности баттл, суссекс, Англия.
